verses.text: The LORD: I have called by name Bezaleel of Judah. I have filled him with the Spirit of God — wisdom, understanding, knowledge, all workmanship — in gold, silver, brass, stones, timber. With him is Aholiab of Dan; in the wise hearted I have put wisdom — that they make all I commanded. Guidance: The first person filled with the Spirit in Scripture is a craftsman. God gives His Spirit not only for prophecy but for skilled hands. Art that serves Him is Spirit-work.

Tabernacle, ark, mercy seat, all furniture, table and vessels, pure candlestick and vessels, altar of incense, altar of burnt offering and vessels, laver and foot, cloths of service, holy garments for Aaron and his sons, anointing oil, sweet incense — they shall do all I have commanded. Guidance: Every piece listed once more. God repeats Himself so the craftsmen will not forget what their hands are to build. Holy work begins by listening twice.

The LORD: tell Israel — My sabbaths ye shall keep, a sign between Me and you, that ye may know I sanctify you. Keep the sabbath; it is holy. Defilers shall die; workers cut off. Six days work; the seventh, sabbath of rest, holy to the LORD — perpetual covenant. For in six days the LORD made heaven and earth, on the seventh rested. Guidance: Even in tabernacle-blueprints, God interrupts: keep the sabbath. The work for God must never run over the rest from God.

When He had made an end of communing with Moses upon mount Sinai, He gave him two tables of testimony — tables of stone, written with the finger of God. Guidance: Forty days have passed. God hands Moses the law in stone, written with His own finger. The same finger that drove out demons in Luke 11 carved these words first. God writes carefully.
